problem.title: First Bad Version
problem.description: You are a product manager and currently leading a team to develop a new product. Unfortunately, the latest version of your product fails the quality check.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Binary Search
solutions.description: Since all versions after the first bad one are also bad, the version space is partitioned into [good...good, bad...bad]. Binary search exploits this monotone property: if isBadVersion(mid) is true, the first bad version is at mid or earlier, so we move right to mid; otherwise it must be strictly after mid. This minimises API calls to O(log n).
solutions.code_python:
# The isBadVersion API is already defined.
# def isBadVersion(version: int) -> bool:

def firstBadVersion(n: int) -> int:
    left, right = 1, n
    while left < right:
        mid = left + (right - left) // 2
        if isBadVersion(mid):  # type: ignore[name-defined]
            right = mid
        else:
            left = mid + 1
    return left
